body.question_1: How would you rate the overall quality of teaching in your courses this semester so far?
body.answer_1: Teaching quality in my Journalism program at the School of Communication has been decent overall, though noticeably inconsistent across different modules this semester. Several lecturers deliver material with clarity and genuine enthusiasm, making those sessions highly productive and intellectually stimulating for the class. However, a few courses suffer from disorganized delivery, limited interaction, and minimal feedback on submitted work, which hampers the learning experience considerably. I would raise concerns about the school to standardize teaching quality benchmarks and conduct regular peer evaluations to ensure a more consistent and high-quality educational experience for all students across every module and year level.

body.question_2: How well does the course content align with current industry standards and your career aspirations?
body.answer_2: The Journalism curriculum at the School of Communication appears to lag behind current industry standards in several significant areas that I believe require urgent review and revision. Many courses continue to teach outdated tools and methodologies that are rarely used in professional settings today, leaving students underprepared for actual workplace demands. The curriculum also lacks sufficient elective depth and specialization pathways, limiting students' ability to develop expertise in niche areas of growing importance. I point out the Academic Affairs Office and school leadership to conduct a thorough curriculum audit in close consultation with industry professionals and alumni to ensure course content remains relevant, rigorous, and career-oriented.

body.question_3: How effective are the assessment methods (assignments, quizzes, exams) in evaluating your understanding of the subject matter?
body.answer_3: Assessment practices in my Journalism program at the School of Communication are generally reasonable, though there is meaningful room for improvement in both consistency and transparency. Some modules employ well-designed assessments that genuinely test comprehension and application, while others rely too heavily on high-stakes end-of-semester examinations that do not adequately reflect students' ongoing learning throughout the term. Timely feedback is provided by some lecturers but is inconsistent across the department. Standardizing minimum feedback turnaround times and encouraging a more diverse portfolio of assessment types would significantly improve the learning experience for all students.

body.question_4: How would you describe the availability and responsiveness of your lecturers outside of scheduled class time?
body.answer_4: Lecturer availability and responsiveness at the School of Communication has been one of the strongest aspects of my Journalism student experience this semester, and I genuinely recognize it. Faculty members consistently maintain clearly communicated office hours, respond to email inquiries within one to two business days, and frequently offer additional consultation sessions during busy assessment periods. The approachable attitude of lecturers creates a supportive learning environment where students feel comfortable raising questions, discussing challenges, and seeking guidance on both academic work and career-related concerns. This level of accessibility significantly enhances engagement and outcomes and is something I hope will be maintained and encouraged across all departments.

body.question_5: How satisfied are you with the learning resources and materials provided for your courses this semester?
body.answer_5: The learning materials provided for several of my Journalism courses at the School of Communication are inconsistent and fall short of what students need to succeed academically. Required textbooks are outdated and frequently out of stock in both the campus bookstore and the library, forcing students to source materials independently at considerable expense and inconvenience. The Learning Management System is inconsistently used by lecturers — some post materials days after class, and some modules have no digital resources whatsoever. I strongly raise concerns about the Library Services and IT Support Department to collaborate on ensuring all course resources are accessible digitally, current, and available before the semester commences.

body.question_6: How would you rate the IT infrastructure and digital learning tools provided by the university to support your studies?
body.answer_6: The IT infrastructure at the School of Communication, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a fair level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_7: How efficient have you found the academic administrative processes such as registration, grade appeals, and scheduling?
body.answer_7: The academic administrative processes managed by the Academic Affairs Office at the School of Communication have been superb this semester, and I recognize the efficiency and transparency with which they are handled. Course registration was smooth and intuitive via the student portal, with clear instructions provided well in advance of enrollment deadlines. Grade appeals and transcript requests are processed promptly and communicated through official channels with regular status updates that reduce student anxiety. Administrative staff are approachable, knowledgeable, and genuinely helpful when students encounter difficulties navigating university policies or procedures. The overall administrative experience reflects a student-centered approach that I believe enhances the broader university experience significantly.

body.question_8: How would you evaluate the quality and accessibility of student support services currently available at this university?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Communication are decent in their scope and delivery, covering the essential categories but lacking depth and proactivity in several important areas that affect student well-being. Counseling services exist and are functional, but appointment availability is limited and wait times are often longer than appropriate for students in need. Financial support processes are reasonably accessible but require significant paperwork and involve delays that reduce their effectiveness in urgent situations. Investing in additional counseling staff, simplifying support request processes, and proactively promoting available services — especially during high-stress periods like mid-terms — would markedly improve the student experience.

body.question_9: How well does the university's physical environment and facilities support your daily learning experience?
body.answer_9: The physical facilities at the School of Communication, under the responsibility of the Facilities Management, present concerning conditions that negatively impact the daily learning experience of students. Several lecture halls have malfunctioning air conditioning systems that make studying in warm months genuinely uncomfortable and distracting. Toilet facilities in older buildings are poorly maintained and frequently reported without timely resolution from the facilities team. Study spaces are chronically insufficient relative to the student population, particularly during examination periods when demand peaks significantly. I urge the university to prioritize facilities maintenance budgets, establish a transparent issue-reporting system, and expand study space capacity as part of its next capital improvement plan.

body.question_10: What specific improvements would you most like to see implemented for the remainder of this semester?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the Journalism student experience at the School of Communication center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only adequate outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
